steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Let the width be $w$ cm. The problem states the length is 38 cm greater than the width, so length $= w + 38$.

explanation:
• Choice A — ‘$A=(w)\left(\frac{w}{38}\right)$’: This makes length $= w/38$, a fraction of the width - the opposite relationship from what is stated. Incorrect
  • Choice B — ‘$A=(w)(38w)$’: This makes length $= 38w$, meaning 38 times the width, not 38 more than the width. Incorrect
  • Choice C — ‘$A=(w)(w+38)$’: Length $= w + 38$ (38 greater than the width); area $= w(w+38)$. This matches exactly. Correct
  • Choice D — ‘$A=w^2$’: This requires length $= w$, i.e., a square. But the block is not a square - the length exceeds the width by 38 cm. Incorrect
That confirms C is correct.
